_comment: Example: Type 2 diabetes treated as a nested control systems failure.
_comment2: Failure mode: cross_layer_override — chronic stress (L4/L6) drives insulin resistance (L3).
_comment3: Correct intervention plan addresses ALL relevant layers (L3 + L4 + L5 + L6).
claim: Type 2 diabetes remission requires a multi-layer intervention that addresses the cross_layer_override driving insulin resistance, not pharmacology alone.

causality.mechanism_detail: Chronic cortisol elevation (L4 HPA axis) suppresses GLUT4 translocation (L2 cellular) and drives hepatic glucose output (L3 organ), overwhelming pancreatic compensation until beta-cell exhaustion. Environmental food access and occupational stress (L6) sustain the cortisol load.

BIO_CONTROL.upper_layer_driver_detail: HPA axis dysregulation (L4) driven by chronic occupational stress + poor sleep (L5) + adverse food environment (L6). Intervention plan includes structured sleep protocol (L5), stress-reduction program (L4), dietary environment redesign (L6), alongside metformin (L1) and insulin sensitization (L3).

intervention_plan.L3_organ: GLP-1 agonist (incretin effect, beta-cell rest, partial setpoint reset)
intervention_plan.L4_whole_body: circadian rhythm restoration via structured sleep + light therapy; HPA axis adaptogenic support
intervention_plan.L5_behavioral: structured resistance exercise (GLUT4 upregulation), dietary pattern change (Mediterranean), sleep hygiene
intervention_plan.L6_social_environmental: food environment audit; occupational stress reduction; social support network

BIO_CONTROL.rationale: Pharmacology alone (L1/L3) addresses the actuator failure but not the upper-layer cortisol override. Without resolving L4/L5/L6 drivers, drug tolerance and relapse are expected within 12-18 months per setpoint defense mechanism.
